step_by_step_thinking:

A thinner mix leads to increased setting expansion primarily because of decreased particle interaction and potential microstructure changes during the setting process. Even though traditional wisdom suggests that excess water might cause issues like poor handling characteristics or delayed setting times, under certain circumstances—and particularly regarding dimensional stability—the outcome could involve measurable expansion rather than just shrinkage. So yes, option C seems accurate.

A thinner mix leads to increased setting expansion primarily because of decreased particle interaction and potential microstructure changes during the setting process. Even though traditional wisdom suggests that excess water might cause issues like poor handling characteristics or delayed setting times, under certain circumstances—and particularly regarding dimensional stability—the outcome could involve measurable expansion rather than just shrinkage. So yes, option C seems accurate.
